text.title: PHP Eloquent ORM Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for idiomatic Eloquent usage.

rule.statement: Every Eloquent model MUST declare `$fillable` (whitelist) OR `$guarded` (blacklist). Models with neither (mass-assignable by default) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without mass-assignment guards, attacker-controlled keys (is_admin, balance) reach Model::create from raw request input.

rule.statement: Attributes with non-string semantics (booleans, dates, arrays, enums, money values) MUST be declared in `$casts`.
rule.rationale: Without casts the attribute is returned as string; `$user->active === true` silently fails.

rule.statement: Service methods returning collections that will be iterated MUST eager-load every association used in the loop via `with()` or `load()`. Lazy access inside the loop is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Lazy access inside a loop produces N+1 queries; one of the most common performance regressions.

rule.statement: Any WHERE clause used in ≥2 call sites MUST be extracted to a query scope (`scopeActive`, `scopeRecent`).
rule.rationale: Duplicated WHERE drifts; scope names give business meaning and centralize change.

rule.statement: Any code path with ≥2 Eloquent writes (save/create/update/delete) MUST wrap them in `DB::transaction(...)` OR explicit `DB::beginTransaction/commit/rollBack`.
rule.rationale: Mid-operation failure leaves partial state; transaction is the unit-of-work boundary.
